Title: The complete synthetic pathway of echinacoside from Cistanche deserticola and its de novo biosynthesis in yeast

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Used in treatment of neurodegenerative disorders, ischemic brain injury, and cancer
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports CdP450 activity during conversion of osmanthuside B to acteoside in reconstructed pathway
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: p-coumaric acid supplementation improved downstream production in sg-1
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtCPR was co-expressed with CdP450 in yeast. 2. It functioned as the redox partner needed for downstream hydroxylation toward acteoside/ECH production.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway reconstruction in yeast and product detection by HPLC
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Support protein rather than direct catalytic step forming ECH
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful accessory gene for enabling plant P450 function in microbial hosts

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Used in treatment of neurodegenerative disorders, ischemic brain injury, and cancer
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides UDP-rhamnose supply required for CdRHT-mediated osmanthuside B synthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvRhm was introduced because S. cerevisiae cannot convert UDP-glucose to UDP-rhamnose. 2. Its inclusion enabled CdRHT-dependent osmanthuside B production in yeast.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway reconstruction in yeast and metabolite analysis by HPLC
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cofactor/sugar-donor supply gene rather than native ECH pathway enzyme from C. deserticola
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important enabling module for microbial biosynthesis of rhamnosylated phenylethanol glycosides

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Used in treatment of neurodegenerative disorders, ischemic brain injury, and cancer
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Amplified at TRP2 and NTS loci in engineered yeast
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Boosted p-coumaric acid precursor production for ECH pathway reconstruction
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous p-coumaric acid supplementation still needed in sg-1 before full de novo route was optimized
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CoTAL was introduced and amplified to enhance p-coumaric acid formation. 2. This precursor-engineering step supported downstream ECH pathway assembly in yeast.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast engineering and HPLC quantification of p-coumaric acid
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used for chassis optimization rather than direct ECH-forming reaction
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supply module for de novo microbial production of phenylpropanoid-derived compounds

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Used in treatment of neurodegenerative disorders, ischemic brain injury, and cancer
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Multiple copies inserted in engineered yeast
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhanced aromatic precursor flux contributing to salidroside and p-coumaric acid supply
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Multiple copies of ARO4K229L were inserted to increase aromatic pathway flux. 2. This supported precursor availability for ECH biosynthesis in yeast.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metabolic engineering and HPLC metabolite measurement
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Host engineering gene, not a native C. deserticola ECH enzyme
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for shikimate-pathway flux enhancement in microbial production systems

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Used in treatment of neurodegenerative disorders, ischemic brain injury, and cancer
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Multiple copies inserted in engineered yeast
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhanced aromatic precursor flux for downstream p-coumaric acid/salidroside production and ECH pathway operation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Multiple copies of ARO7G141S were introduced in yeast precursor pathway engineering. 2. This supported de novo ECH biosynthesis by improving aromatic metabolic flux.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metabolic engineering and HPLC metabolite measurement
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Host engineering gene, not a native C. deserticola ECH enzyme
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for improving phenylpropanoid precursor supply in yeast

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Used in treatment of neurodegenerative disorders, ischemic brain injury, and cancer
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provided a high-activity alternative enzyme for osmanthuside A generation in the reconstructed yeast pathway toward ECH
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SiAT1 was selected from reported candidate enzymes because it showed the highest activity for osmanthuside A generation. 2. Its use enabled efficient osmanthuside B production in the yeast pathway.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway reconstruction in yeast and HPLC product analysis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Non-native substitute used instead of CdHCT in microbial pathway optimization
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful alternative acyltransferase for improving microbial production efficiency

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Used in treatment of neurodegenerative disorders, ischemic brain injury, and cancer
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provided hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA donor supply in optimized yeast osmanthuside B pathway
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pt4CL1 was co-introduced with SiAT1 into yeast for osmanthuside B pathway construction. 2. It supported precursor activation for downstream ECH pathway assembly.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway reconstruction in yeast and HPLC product analysis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Non-native substitute used for pathway optimization; source species not clearly specified in provided text
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful alternative 4CL for optimizing microbial production

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Used in treatment of neurodegenerative disorders, ischemic brain injury, and cancer
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Echinacoside biosynthesis / phenylpropanoid-derived phenylethanol glycoside pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA supply for osmanthuside A branch
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AMP-binding enzyme family; 4CL with conserved domain I (AMP-binding site) and domain II (catalytic site)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Caffeic acid; p-coumaric acid; CoA; ATP
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamic acids by ligating CoA to form hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA thioesters used for acyl transfer
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key precursor-supplying step; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified from transcriptome candidates; cloned based on relatively high expression; functionally linked with callus-assisted screening
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: C. deserticola fleshy stem tissues and calli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides activated p-coumaroyl/caffeoyl donors for downstream osmanthuside A formation and thereby supports ECH biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Callus culture and 6-BA-assisted transcriptome mining facilitated identification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Unigene 129456 was identified and named Cd4CL1. 2. Cd4CL1 showed activity toward caffeic acid, evidenced by color change/absorbance shift and downstream coupled validation. 3. Cd4CL1 supplies hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA donors required for osmanthuside A and ultimately echinacoside biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate screening from transcriptomics; gene cloning; in vitro enzyme assay; coupled reaction with hydroxycinnamoyltransferase; product analysis by HPLC/LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originally one of 15 4CL candidates; cloned from Unigene 129456; high similarity to Striga 4CLs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for pathway reconstruction and precursor boosting in microbial or plant metabolic engineering of echinacoside/related PhGs

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Cistanche deserticola; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Cistanche deserticola; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Used in treatment of neurodegenerative disorders, ischemic brain injury, and cancer
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Echinacoside biosynthesis / phenylpropanoid-derived phenylethanol glycoside pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers p-coumaroyl or caffeoyl group from the corresponding CoA thioester to salidroside to form acylated phenylethanol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key committed entry into characterized downstream ECH pathway; not formally established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Very low in plant samples (FPKM 0.01–0.85) but high in calli (124.77–280)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: C. deserticola fleshy stem tissues and calli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes formation of osmanthuside A from salidroside, establishing a direct route toward ECH biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low expression in planta may complicate native-pathway enhancement
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Callus in vitro culture induced expression and enabled discovery
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Unigene 16544 was identified as CdHCT. 2. CdHCT converted salidroside plus p-coumaroyl-CoA or caffeoyl-CoA into osmanthuside A and syringalide A. 3. Its expression was strongly induced in calli relative to plant tissues, highlighting callus-assisted pathway discovery in the parasitic plant.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome/WGCNA candidate mining; cloning; heterologous expression; in vitro coupled enzyme assay; HPLC and LC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CL10082 had highest transcript abundance but was inactive in tested reaction; CdHCT shared 88.17% similarity with a Phtheirospermum japonicum acyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical enzyme to establish the first dedicated acylation step for ECH/related PhG biosynthesis in engineered hosts

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Cistanche deserticola; Pichia pastoris; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Cistanche deserticola; Pichia pastoris; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Used in treatment of neurodegenerative disorders, ischemic brain injury, and cancer
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Rhamnosylation branch from osmanthuside A to osmanthuside B
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers rhamnose to osmanthuside A to produce osmanthuside B
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key pathway step; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in plant samples during seed-setting and succulent stem growth stages; CL2720 upregulated in 6-BA-treated calli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pGAP in P. pastoris for heterologous expression; yeast engineering promoters not individually specified
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: C. deserticola fleshy stem tissues and calli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables conversion of osmanthuside A to osmanthuside B, a key intermediate toward acteoside and ECH
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Annotation suggested anthocyanin-related function, implying possible competing native roles
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CL2720 was identified as CdRHT. 2. CdRHT converted osmanthuside A to osmanthuside B in coupled assays with UDP-rhamnose. 3. Introducing CdRHT with VvRhm enabled osmanthuside B production in engineered yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Differential expression analysis; cloning; heterologous expression in Pichia pastoris; coupled enzyme assay; HPLC/LC-MS; pathway reconstruction in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also produced syringalide A 2'-α-L-rhamnopyranoside from syringalide A; closest homolog from Phtheirospermum japonicum with 91.45% similarity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential for assembling ECH and related phenylethanol glycoside pathways in microbial chassis

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Used in treatment of neurodegenerative disorders, ischemic brain injury, and cancer
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Hydroxylation from osmanthuside B to acteoside
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450; most similar to p-coumarate-3-hydroxylase-like enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates osmanthuside B to form acteoside
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key pathway step; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Selected from highly expressed CYP candidates during succulent stem growth; expression increased in 6-BA- and phenylalanine-treated calli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: C. deserticola fleshy stem tissues and calli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts osmanthuside B to acteoside, enabling the final glucosylation step to ECH
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dependence on redox partner in heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Succulent stem growth period used for candidate mining; 6-BA and phenylalanine increased expression in calli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UniGene 120362 was identified as CdP450. 2. Expression in osmanthuside B-producing yeast generated a new product identified as acteoside. 3. CdP450 expression was elevated in elicited calli and was necessary for downstream ECH pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression-guided candidate screening; cloning; heterologous expression in yeast; metabolite profiling by HPLC and LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Most similar to p-coumarate-3-hydroxylase from Rehmannia glutinosa (87.65%)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary hydroxylation module for complete ECH biosynthesis in engineered hosts

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Cistanche deserticola; Pichia pastoris; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Cistanche deserticola; Pichia pastoris; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Used in treatment of neurodegenerative disorders, ischemic brain injury, and cancer
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal glucosylation step from acteoside to echinacoside
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to acteoside to generate echinacoside
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low across growth stages but relatively higher during succulent stem growth; induced among callus genes responding to phenylalanine and salidroside
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pGAP in P. pastoris for enzyme testing; yeast engineering promoters not individually specified
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: C. deserticola fleshy stem tissues and calli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly catalyzes the final conversion of acteoside to echinacoside
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low native expression may constrain plant accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phenylalanine and salidroside treatments highlighted candidate expression in calli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UniGene 46335 was identified as CdUGT. 2. CdUGT was the only tested UGT that converted acteoside to echinacoside. 3. This newly identified enzyme enabled the final biosynthetic step and completion of de novo ECH synthesis in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Differential expression analysis; cloning; heterologous expression; in vitro assay with acteoside and UDP-glucose; HPLC/LC-MS/NMR; pathway reconstruction in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: First reported UGT directly applied to ECH production; closest homolog was a scopoletin glucosyltransferase from Phtheirospermum japonicum (64.88%)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value terminal enzyme for metabolic engineering and pathway completion in microbial production systems
